title: dionysia revoluta subsp. revoluta
intro: Dionysia revoluta subsp. revoluta, also known as the dwarf dionysia or Persian stonecrop, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, native to high-altitude mountainous regions of Iran and Afghanistan. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Dionysia revoluta subsp. revoluta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide protection against oxidative stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosages for human consumption.
